description_long: This object consists of three irregularly shaped rocks clustered together, each with a rugged, natural appearance. The surfaces are uneven, with visible crevices, bumps, and subtle color variations ranging from medium to dark gray, interspersed with faint greenish moss or lichen patches. The largest rock is elongated and slightly sloped, while the two smaller rocks are more rounded and squat, positioned adjacent to the main mass. The texture appears coarse and weathered, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. The rocks have a matte, non-reflective finish, and their edges are blunt rather than sharp. This formation would be typical as a landscaping or barrier feature in an urban park or along a sidewalk.
description: A cluster of three rugged, gray rocks with coarse, weathered surfaces and subtle greenish patches, arranged in a naturalistic grouping. The largest rock is elongated, with two smaller, rounded rocks beside it.
description_view_0: The largest rock is seen in profile, with its elongated shape and two smaller rocks clearly visible to the right.
description_view_1: The rocks are viewed head-on, showing the rounded faces of the two smaller rocks in front of the main mass.
description_view_2: The elongated side of the largest rock is prominent, with one smaller rock to the left and the other partially obscured.
description_view_3: The cluster is viewed from the rear, highlighting the pointed end of the largest rock and the grouping's overall triangular silhouette.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
